zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William Dormer, KB, MP於1514年出生在英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾時，他的父親Sir Robert Dormer29歲，母親Jane Newdegate18歲。他於大約1550年與Lady Dorothy Catesby在英國結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和10個女兒的父母。他去世於1575年5月17日在英國巴金哈姆爾文享年61歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾艾勒斯波利 瓦爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William Dormer, KB, MP于1514年出生在英国英格兰巴金哈姆爾时，他的父亲Sir Robert Dormer29岁，母亲Jane Newdegate18岁。他于大约1550年与Lady Dorothy Catesby在英国结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和10个女儿的父母。他去世于1575年5月17日在英国巴金哈姆爾文享年61岁，埋葬在英国英格兰巴金哈姆爾艾勒斯波利 瓦爾。

nameMeanings.name: Dormer,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): nickname for a lazy man or a sleepyhead from Old French dormeur ‘sleeper, sluggard’ (from Latin dormitor, from dormire ‘to sleep’).

Irish (Kilkenny): when not of the same origin as 1 above, this is a shortened Anglicized form of the Donegal name Ó Díorma, a shortened form of Ó Duibhdhíormaigh ‘descendant of Duibhdhíormach’, a personal name composed of Gaelic dubh ‘black’ + díormach ‘trooper’.
German (Dörmer): occupational name for a watchman on a tower (of a castle or town), from an agent derivative of Middle Low German torn or Middle High German turn ‘tower’. Compare Dermer and Dorner .

variants.name: Dermer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Hersh, Pinchas, Yaffa.
German: from an old personal name, Terrimar, a variant of Därr (see Darr ).
German: variant of Dörmer (see Dormer ), an occupational name for a watchman, someone who manned a tower. Compare Derner .

variants.name: Dearmond
meanings: Irish: variant of Dermond, a shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Duibhdhiorma ‘descendant of Duibhdhiorma’ a personal name meaning ‘black trooper’ (see Dormer 2). There has been some confusion between the personal names Duibhdhiorma and Diarmaid, so that Dearmond alternates with McDermott . See also Dearman 2.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Dormer, KB, MP was born in 1514, in Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Robert Dormer, was 29 and his mother, Jane Newdegate, was 18. He married Lady Dorothy Catesby about 1550, in England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 10 daughters. He died on 17 May 1575, in Wing, Buckinghamshire, England, at the age of 61, and was buried in Aylesbury Vale, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom.

Il a épousé Lady Dorothy Catesby environ 1550, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 10 filles. Il est décédé le 17 mai 1575, à Wing, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 61 ans, et a été enterré à Aylesbury Vale, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Dormer, KB, MP(이)가 1514년 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Robert Dormer(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Jane Newdegate(은)는 18세였다. 그는 대략 1550년 영국에서 Lady Dorothy Catesby(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 열 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1575년 5월 17일 영국 벅잉함실르 윙에서 61세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르 아일레스버이 발에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William Dormer, KB, MP родился в 1514 г. в местности буккингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir Robert Dormer было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Jane Newdegate, 18. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Dorothy Catesby около 1550 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 10 дочерей. Он умер 17 мая 1575 г. в местности виг, буккингхамшир, Англия в возрасте 61 год и был похоронен в местности аилесбури веил, буккингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания.

Se casó con Lady Dorothy Catesby aproximadamente 1550, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 10 hijas. Murió el 17 de mayo de 1575, en Wing, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 61 años, y fue sepultado en Aylesbury Vale, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
